ttnn_inventory_method: import ttnn; dir(ttnn|ttnn.experimental|ttnn.transformer|ttnn.experimental.ccl) + grep of gpt_oss/deepseek_v3/qwen3_vl/llama3_70b_galaxy/tt_transformers reference models

missing_ops.name: sparse_lightning_attention
missing_ops.why_missing: Learned lightning-indexer + block-sparse GQA SDPA. The indexer (index_q_proj/index_k_proj 4 heads x dim128 + per-head index_q_norm/index_k_norm + partial rope) scores idx_q.idx_k^T, max-pools over a 128-wide key block then over index heads (amax/amax) to produce per-(query,block) scores, force-boosts the local block(s) to +inf, top-16 selects blocks, then builds a block-selection AND token-causal additive mask that gates a normal GQA SDPA. No ttnn primitive composes a LEARNED block selector into the SDPA mask; ttnn SDPA variants (scaled_dot_product_attention, paged_*, windowed_*, flash_mla) only take dense/causal/paged/sliding-window masks, none take a per-query learned top-k-block index set. The amax-over-block + amax-over-index-head reduction, the +inf local-block scatter, the top-k-block selection with -1 left-packing, and the repeat_interleave block->token mask expansion are not expressible as a single ttnn op and the fused indexer kernel has no analogue.
missing_ops.tile_alignment_notes: head_dim 128 and index_head_dim 128 are tile-aligned (multiples of 32). block_size 128 is tile-aligned. partial rope rotary_dim 64 is tile-aligned. The non-trivial part is the dynamic top-16-of-N-blocks gather and the block->token mask expansion, not tile alignment. S_k must be padded up to a 128 multiple (-inf pad) before block reshape; that pad is already tile-aligned.
missing_ops.spec_ref: see sparse_lightning_attention.reference.notes (FULL tt-lang spec in git notes) and moe_decoder_layer.reference.notes
missing_ops.bringup_fallback: Per ARCHITECTURE.md / use_case hybrid_notes: layers 3..59 may run FULL dense-causal GQA (gqa_attention) for numerical parity in first bring-up, accepting reduced long-context efficiency; the indexer only prunes keys, so dense GQA is a strict superset and PCC-equivalent when no block is dropped.

missing_ops.name: vision_rope_3d
missing_ops.why_missing: 3D RoPE over (temporal,H,W) axes with a shared inv_freq table multiplied by each axis coordinate, banded as [T13|H13|W13|T13|H13|W13] (rot_dim 78), 2 tail channels never rotated, NeoX half-split, and spatial-merge (m=2) coordinate reordering (reshape(h//m,m,w//m,m).permute(0,2,1,3).flatten) plus temporal repeat_interleave. ttnn's rotary ops (ttnn.experimental.rotary_embedding_llama / rotary_embedding_hf) are 1D llama/NeoX-style: they apply a single per-position cos/sin table; they have NO notion of a 3-axis banded table or spatial-merge coordinate generation. NOTE: the qwen3_vl reference proves the cos/sin TABLE is built host-side (compute_gather_cos_sin -> precompute_freqs/gather_cos_sin in plain torch) and only the APPLY runs on device. So the band/coord/passthrough logic is a host-side table builder; the on-device apply can reuse rotary_embedding_llama with a padded head_dim. The 'missing' work is the custom 3D table builder + ensuring the 78-rot/2-pass split is honoured.
missing_ops.tile_alignment_notes: head_dim 80 is NOT a multiple of 32 -> pad to padded_head_dim=96 (ceil(80/32)*32), EXACTLY the documented qwen3_vl/tt/vision_attention.py workaround (pads q/k/v/out weights and rope cos/sin mats to 96; comment in that file: 'workaround until rotary embeddings support sub-tile head dims'). rot_dim 78 is also sub-tile; pad the rotated band cos/sin to 96 with cos=1/sin=0 in the 78..96 tail so the pad and the 2 genuine passthrough channels are identity. So head_dim=80 is a tile-alignment WORKAROUND (already solved in-repo), NOT a true missing op. The only genuinely new artifact is the 3-axis/spatial-merge HOST table builder feeding the existing device rotary apply.
missing_ops.spec_ref: see vision_rope_3d.reference.notes (TT-LANG SPEC: 3D rope head_dim80, 78 rotated(3x26)+2 passthrough, band [T|H|W|T|H|W], smerge2 coords, NeoX half-split)

compose_ok.name: rms_norm
compose_ok.notes: Gemma (1+w) scale: precompute (1+w) host-side OR add 1.0 on device; ttnn.rms_norm exists. fp32 reduce.

compose_ok.name: vision_layernorm
compose_ok.notes: true LayerNorm with bias eps1e-5; ttnn.layer_norm supports weight+bias.

compose_ok.name: embedding
compose_ok.notes: plain row lookup, no scaling; sharded vocab.

compose_ok.name: rope
compose_ok.notes: Partial rope rotary_dim64<head_dim128, NeoX half-split, theta5e6: rotate the 0:64 slice, pass through 64:128 and concat -- EXACTLY the deepseek_v3/tt/rope.py MLA partial-rope pattern (rope dim = qk_rope_head_dim). cos/sin table built host-side. 64 is tile-aligned.

compose_ok.name: patch_embedding
compose_ok.notes: Conv3d kernel==stride exactly tiles each [C*tps*p*p]=1176-flat patch -> mathematically a LINEAR proj onto 1280 (reference.notes: '==linear on flat patch'). ttnn has NO conv3d (confirmed dir(ttnn): conv3d=False, only Conv3dConfig exists for tt_dit), but it is NOT needed: realize as a matmul/linear over flattened patches (no bias). host_resident allowed=true (tiny, run-once).

compose_ok.name: qk_norm
compose_ok.notes: per-head Gemma RMSNorm over head_dim128 (tile-aligned), weight[128] broadcast across heads; reuses rms_norm. pre-rope.

compose_ok.name: gqa_attention
compose_ok.notes: 64q/4kv head_dim128 (tile-aligned), per-head qknorm, partial rope, causal SDPA scale 1/sqrt128. KV replicated x8 per TP plan so SDPA stays chip-local (repeat_kv folded into placement). Standard llama3_70b_galaxy GQA idiom. o_proj row-parallel -> all_reduce.

compose_ok.name: vision_attention
compose_ok.notes: 16-head MHA (no GQA, no qknorm), non-causal, q/k/v/out bias, scale 1/sqrt80, 3D rope on q/k. head_dim80 padded->96 (qwen3_vl workaround). Reuses qwen3_vl/tt/vision_attention.py wholesale; only the rope table differs (vision_rope_3d).

compose_ok.name: swigluoai_mlp
compose_ok.notes: EXACT op sequence already implemented in gpt_oss/tt/experts/prefill.py: gate=clamp(max=limit7); glu=gate*sigmoid(gate*alpha1.702); out=down((up.clamp(+-7)+1)*glu). No new op.

compose_ok.name: moe_gate
compose_ok.notes: Router: linear 6144->128, sigmoid scores, +e_score_correction_bias for SELECTION only, top4, normalize, *2.0. Same composed op family as deepseek_v3/tt/deepseek_moe_gate/op.py (DeepseekMoeGateOp is a DEMO-level composition, not a ttnn primitive; enable_sigmoid + add_score_correction_bias + scaling). MiniMax diffs are value-only (scaling 2.0 not 2.5, no grouped/topk_group routing). topk over 128 -> top4 supported by ttnn.topk.

compose_ok.name: moe_experts
compose_ok.notes: 128 routed experts, each SwiGLU-OAI inter3072. gpt_oss/tt/experts already does expert-parallel swiglu-oai compute + gather/scatter + down-proj all-reduce. expert-parallel ~4 experts/chip per TP plan. x2.0 folded into gate weights. No new op.

compose_ok.name: shared_expert
compose_ok.notes: bare SwiGLU-OAI inter3072 on all tokens, NO gate/scale, added to routed output. Identical to swigluoai_mlp. deepseek_v3 shared-expert path.

compose_ok.name: vision_mlp
compose_ok.notes: 1280->5120 erf-GELU ->1280, both bias. ttnn.gelu erf mode + ttnn.erf available. qwen3_vl/tt/vision_mlp.py.

compose_ok.name: dense_decoder_layer
compose_ok.notes: pre-norm GQA + dense SwiGLU-OAI inter12288, two gemma RMS norms, plain residual adds. All deps compose. llama3_70b_galaxy decoder idiom.

compose_ok.name: moe_decoder_layer
compose_ok.notes: pre-norm sparse-lightning-attn + (routed experts + shared) MoE. COMPOSITE: itself only does norms+residual adds+sum, but it DEPENDS on the MISSING sparse_lightning_attention. With the documented full-GQA fallback this layer is fully composable; with true sparse attn it transitively needs the authored op. Marked compose (its own body adds nothing new).

compose_ok.name: vision_encoder_layer
compose_ok.notes: pre-norm CLIP block ln1->attn(3d rope)->res->ln2->mlp->res. Composes; depends on vision_rope_3d (compose). qwen3_vl/tt/vision_block.py.

compose_ok.name: patch_merge_mlp
compose_ok.notes: reshape [N,6144]->[N/4,24576] row-major then linear24576->6144 ->gelu-> linear6144->6144. host_resident allowed. qwen3_vl/tt/patch_merger.py.

compose_ok.name: multimodal_projector
compose_ok.notes: linear1280->6144 ->gelu-> linear6144->6144, bias. host_resident allowed. qwen3_vl/tt/patch_merger.py.

compose_ok.name: vision_encoder
compose_ok.notes: COMPOSITE tower: conv3d(=linear) patch_embed -> pre_layrnorm -> 32x encoder layers -> projector -> patch_merge. Adds no new op of its own; depends on vision_rope_3d (compose, with host table builder). Marked compose.

compose_ok.name: final_norm
compose_ok.notes: gemma RMSNorm before lm_head; thin wrapper over rms_norm.

compose_ok.name: lm_head
compose_ok.notes: bias-free 6144->200064, untied, column-parallel sharded + all_gather logits. deepseek_v3/tt/lm_head1d.py.

out_of_scope.name: mtp_head
out_of_scope.why: config declares num_mtp_modules=7 but NO mtp/nextn weights in the checkpoint safetensors index (0 of 23416 keys). Not buildable from this snapshot; dropped (not required for correctness). deepseek_v3/tt/mtp.py is the architectural reference if weights are ever sourced. NOT counted as a missing op to author.
